bio: Hossein Taeb headed the [IRGC](/orgs/islamic-revolutionary-guard-corps)'s Intelligence Organisation for over thirteen years (2009–2022), making him one of the most feared figures in Iran's security apparatus. A student of [Ali Khamenei](/people/ali-khamenei) and wartime associate of [Mojtaba Khamenei](/people/mojtaba-khamenei) in the Habib Battalion, he combined IRGC and MOIS experience spanning from the [1988 massacre of political prisoners](/events/mass-executions-1988) through the [chain murders of 1998](/events/chain-murders-iran-1998) to the suppression of the [2009 Green Movement](/events/green-movement-protests-2009) and [2022 Mahsa Amini protests](/events/mahsa-amini-protests-2022). He was dismissed in June 2022 following a series of Israeli intelligence successes inside Iran, including the assassination of nuclear scientists, and a foiled plot against Israeli tourists in Turkey that exposed IRGC-IO networks. He was replaced by General Mohammad Kazemi.
editorial_notes: Born 1963. IRGC-IO chief October 2009–June 2022. Basij commander 2007–2009. Previously in MOIS under Fallahian. US and EU sanctioned.
